description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a roughly rectangular profile and a visibly stratified composition. The upper surface is relatively flat and smooth, while the sides and bottom are rugged and uneven, showing a mix of fine-grained and coarse aggregate textures. One face features a yellow painted marking, while another displays handwritten black text, suggesting it may have been cataloged or used for identification. The coloration ranges from dark gray to brown, with lighter patches and embedded pebbles visible in the matrix. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or significant fractures. Its overall appearance suggests it was quarried or broken from a larger mass and may serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment.
description: A dense, irregular stone with a flat top, rugged sides, and visible aggregate textures, marked with yellow paint and black writing.
description_view_0: The main face shows a flat, smooth upper surface with a yellow painted marking and a rough, aggregate-rich lower half.
description_view_1: A narrow side view reveals the stone's thickness and the transition from smooth to rough textures.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face displays black handwritten text and a mix of embedded pebbles and fine matrix.
description_view_3: Another narrow side view highlights the stone's layered structure and rough, uneven surface.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a boundary marker
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and layering with visible aggregate inclusions; painted and written markings add artificial patterning.
attribute_weathering: Partially weathered with rough, chipped surfaces and some smoother, freshly broken areas; paint and ink markings are intact.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or built environments but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; markings make it stand out visually.
